summary: v1.11.0 backend testing complete. All primary + regression Node ESM test suites passed. Syntax checks clean. Independent verification of VISCA bytes, IRIS_FSTOP lookup, expCompDirect encoding, and variables.js registrations all pass.
critical_code_review_comments: commands.js, actions.js, main.js, variables.js all pass `node --check` cleanly.
IRIS_FSTOP is a plain frozen-style array export; all 14 indices match user spec exactly.
expCompDirect encoding is correct: display value 3 -> raw 10 -> byte 7 (payload low nibble) = 0x0A. High/low nibble split handled properly.
NDI variant routing in gain_up/down/reset/rotary_* correctly redirects to state.expComp and publishes exposure_compensation; standard variant untouched. Verified by test 9 + 11.
Clamps at +/-7 for expComp and 0..13 for iris are enforced synchronously in the action callbacks — no drift observed.
iris_up/down/direct now publishes both `iris` (raw index) and `iris_fstop` (label) atomically; prior test suites already updated to expect both, no regression.
seed_data_creation: None. Tests use in-process mocks (mock-camera.js + mock ModuleInstance).
context_for_next_testing_agent: v1.11.0 fully green. Test totals: expcomp-iris 43/43, realtime-publish 22/22, focus-tracking 40/40, backlight-focus-home 40/40, axis-home-and-zoom 32/32, step-calibration 26/26, independent-verify all-scenarios pass, independent-axis-home 41/0. Independent v1.11 spot-check script confirms inqExpComp bytes [0x81,0x09,0x04,0x4E,0xFF], IRIS_FSTOP[14] indices, expCompDirect(10) byte 7=0x0A, expCompUp=81 01 04 0E 02 FF, and both new variable IDs registered. Note: variables.js exports `getVariables()` (not getVariableDefinitions) — remember this if writing new spot-check scripts.
